name: snip
description: The visual communication layer between humans and AI agents. Capture, annotate, render diagrams, and organize with AI — powered by Electron and Ollama. macOS & Linux.
readme_summary: Snip is a visual communication tool for AI agents, enabling users to capture, annotate, and render diagrams and previews directly within their AI coding workflows. It integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code to replace textual descriptions with visual outputs, facilitating a more intuitive review and iteration process.
key_features: Visual communication layer for AI agents
Capture, annotate, and render diagrams/previews
Integration with AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
Menu bar / system tray application with CLI
Supports Mermaid diagrams, HTML previews, and image annotation
Screenshot library with search and organization capabilities
OCR for text extraction from images
MCP server for agents without shell access
primary_use_case: To enhance AI coding workflows by enabling visual communication, allowing AI agents to render diagrams and previews and users to annotate and provide feedback directly on these visuals.
cover_image_prompt: A master architect in a futuristic workshop, using a magical tablet to instantly transform abstract blueprints into detailed, glowing 3D models and interactive diagrams. The architect points at specific areas on the tablet, causing those sections to highlight and expand on the main model. Around the workshop, smaller screens display flowing code and data streams. The repository name 'Snip' is subtly etched onto the tablet's frame. The scene is bathed in a soft, ethereal light, emphasizing creativity and innovation. The overall style is a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic.

name: reclip
description: Download videos from almost any website. Lightweight, self-hosted media downloader with a clean web UI.
readme_summary: ReClip is a self-hosted, open-source media downloader that allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites. It features a clean web UI, supports MP4 and MP3 formats, and offers quality selection and bulk download capabilities.
key_features: Download videos from 1000+ supported sites (via yt-dlp)
MP4 video or MP3 audio extraction
Quality/resolution picker
Bulk downloads
Automatic URL deduplication
Clean, responsive UI
Single Python file backend
primary_use_case: Downloading videos and audio from a wide variety of websites through a user-friendly web interface.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot with a large funnel on its head is standing in front of a digital waterfall of video and audio icons flowing from various social media logos (YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Twitter/X). The robot is catching these media streams in a net, transforming them into clean MP4 video files and MP3 audio icons that are neatly organized in a digital basket at its feet. Subtle UI elements showing download progress and format selection are visible on a small screen attached to the robot. The scene is bright and clean, with a modern, flat design aesthetic and a vibrant color palette, emphasizing ease of use and accessibility. The repository name 'ReClip' is subtly integrated into the robot's design.

name: home-gallery
description: Self-hosted open-source web gallery to view your photos and videos featuring mobile-friendly, tagging and AI powered image discovery
readme_summary: HomeGallery is a self-hosted, open-source web gallery designed for personal photo and video collections. It offers features like tagging, mobile-friendliness, and AI-powered image and face discovery to help users browse and rediscover their media archives privately.
key_features: Self-hosted
Open-source
Web gallery
Mobile-friendly
Tagging
AI-powered image discovery
AI-powered face discovery
Geo-reverse lookups
EXIF data support
Calculate previews
Support for Linux, macOS, Windows
Docker image available
primary_use_case: To provide a private, self-hosted, and feature-rich web gallery for users to organize, browse, and discover their personal photo and video collections, with an emphasis on user privacy and mobile accessibility.
cover_image_prompt: A digital archivist, depicted as a friendly librarian, meticulously organizing a vast, glowing library of personal photos and videos. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass that reveals hidden connections and tags within the media, while a subtle AI-powered 'discovery beam' highlights forgotten memories. Input shelves overflow with diverse media sources (hard drives, phones), and output is a beautifully organized, accessible digital gallery displayed on a sleek, modern interface. The scene is set in a cozy, tech-infused study with soft, warm lighting, incorporating subtle UI elements and data particles. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, inviting aesthetic.

name: summarize
description: Point at any URL/YouTube/Podcast or file. Get the gist. CLI and Chrome Extension.
readme_summary: Summarize is a versatile tool that provides concise summaries from various sources including URLs, files, and media like YouTube videos. It offers both a command-line interface (CLI) and a Chrome Side Panel extension for convenient access.
key_features: Chrome Side Panel chat with streaming agent and history
Video slide extraction with OCR and transcript cards for YouTube and video files
Media-aware summarization (auto-detects audio/video vs. page content)
Support for multiple coding CLI backends (Codex, Claude, Gemini, etc.)
Streaming Markdown output with metrics and cache status
CLI support for URLs, files, podcasts, YouTube, audio/video, and PDFs
Transcript-first media processing with fallback transcription services
Support for local, paid, and free summarization models
Multiple output modes including Markdown, JSON, and metrics
primary_use_case: To quickly extract the essential information from web pages, documents, and media content through an accessible CLI and browser extension.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old owl perched on a stack of books and digital devices (laptop, tablet, smartphone). The owl is holding a magnifying glass over a glowing, abstract representation of information, extracting a single, clear beam of light that forms a concise summary. Around the owl, subtle digital particles and faint code snippets float, indicating the technological nature of the process. The background is a softly blurred library or digital workspace, with the repository name 'Summarize' subtly etched into the owl's perch. The overall mood is one of knowledge and efficiency, rendered in a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration style.
